modes.instruction: Orient the reader to the two volumes, the museum layer, and the 10-route seed before opening individual chapters.
Study one chapter through its card, transcript, commentary, limits, and return path.
Generate discussion questions grounded in public card limits and source chapter text.
Use the commentary scaffold as a project-development canvas, not as finished analysis.
Connect chapters to public pattern IDs without importing private strategy notes.
Design or review chapter exhibits through ph-mus schemas and manifest boundaries.
Apply patterns to present-day questions only as orientation and with explicit evidentiary limits.

guardrails: Homer to Tolstoy is the Volume I literary spine, not a side corridor.
sh-16 is a routed Tolstoy endpoint via an Anna Karenina coda, not a dedicated Tolstoy lecture.
ph-mus is not a third volume.
Application routes are public orientation only, not quotation-grade live operational analysis.
Commentaries are open project canvases and should not be treated as final scholarly review.
Public growth goals are ambitions; do not claim views, attention, or launch readiness has already been earned.
do_not_claim: Do not claim this repo is official course material or endorsement.
Do not claim live geopolitical certainty from orientation routes.
Do not claim museum artifact binaries are stored in Git.
Do not claim commentaries are complete final analysis.
Do not claim ph-mus is a third volume.
